fields.summary: SonarQube analysis token expired causing CI pipeline authentication failure
fields.description: All our Jenkins pipelines started failing overnight with the error: ERROR: SonarQube server can not be reached at https://sonar.company.com. Please check the property sonar.host.url. The SonarQube server is up and accessible via browser. The pipelines were working fine until yesterday evening. We use a shared service account token stored in Jenkins credentials to authenticate with SonarQube. This is affecting all 14 microservice pipelines simultaneously.

content.text: Build #1204 FAILED. Stage: SonarQube Analysis. Error: 401 Unauthorized. Token authentication failed.

I tested the token manually using curl and got 401. So the token itself is the issue, not the network or server. The token belongs to the sq-ci-service-account user. Can you check if the token has expired?

Confirmed. The token for sq-ci-service-account was created 90 days ago and our SonarQube instance has a global token expiry policy set to 90 days. The token expired at midnight. I am generating a new token now. Going forward we should either set the token to never expire for CI service accounts or set a calendar reminder 2 weeks before expiry. Generating new token and updating Jenkins credentials now.

New token generated and updated in Jenkins global credentials. All 14 pipelines are now passing SonarQube analysis. Root cause: 90-day token expiry policy applied to service account token. Fix: generated new token with no expiry for CI service accounts. Recommendation: review token expiry policy for all CI service accounts.
